bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueD), which gives me minimum utility (0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total). This is very early (t=0.0), so I should counter with my ideal bid to anchor high. My best offer is all valueA, giving me utility of 0.4*1 + 0.3*1 + 0.2*1 + 0.1*1 = 1.0. I'll start firm at my maximum utility position.

We're very early (t=0.02), so I can afford to stay near my optimal bid. The opponent started at their best (all valueD for them, worst for me) and slightly conceded on issueA to valueC. My highest-weight issue is issueA (0.4), so I should hold firm there. I'll make a small concession on my lowest-weight issue (issueD, weight 0.1) to signal willingness to negotiate, moving from valueA to valueB there, while keeping everything else at valueA.

We're still very early (t=0.04), so I should stay near my top bids.
